ety: for A. فراسة, v.n. fr. tafarrus, 'perceiving a thing intuitively,' &c., inf. n. v of فرس 'to be skilled' (in
def: s.f. Perception or discernment of (one's) internal or intrinsic condition or character, &c. by outward indications; physiognomy; intuitive perception, insight, discernment, sagacity, penetration, acuteness, understanding, judgment.
more: (from فرس Heb. and To divide, to distinguish) s. f. 1. Physiognomy. 2. Penetration, sagacity, understanding.

ety: inf. n. of فرش 'to spread' (a carpet
def: s.m. Spreading; strewing, &c.; — a thing that is spread (a bed, or carpet, &c.)
subs: s.m. A room in which carpets, &c. are kept.

def: s.m. Pharaoh (king of Egypt); — anyone inordinately proud, or insolent, or unbelieving; an audacious and cruel tyrant.
more: n. m. 1. Pharoah. 2. A proud arrogant person; a despot.
(Heb. ) n. prop. 1. Pharaoh. 2. ( met. ) Any cruel tyrant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِرْعَوْن (fir'awn), borrowed from Arabic فِرْعَوْن (firʕawn), possibly borrowed from Classical Syriac ܦܹܪܥܘܼܢ, borrowed from Koine Greek Φαραών (Pharaṓn), borrowed from Biblical Hebrew פַּרְעֹה, borrowed from Egyptian pr-ꜥꜣ, from pr + ꜥꜣ.

ety: firʻaun+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Pride, haughtiness, insolence, arrogance, audaciousness; tyranny.
more: n. f. Haughtiness; arrogance. firāūn-i-be sāmān . 1. An impotent rebel. 2. A little imp; a devil.

ety: A. firdaus, prob. fr. the Pers.; Armen. partez; Heb. pardês; Gr. παράδεισος; Zend pairidaêza (pairi = S. परि; daêza, fr. rt. diz = S. दिह्)
def: s.m. A garden; Paradise
more: (Heb. Aram. Gr. Παραδεισος ) s. m. A garden, paradise. فردوس مکاني firdaus-makān-ī, adj. Whose dwelling is paradise.
subs: adj. Located in paradise, whose dwelling is in paradise, deceased (applied to an emperor or great personage).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فردوس (firdaws), from Arabic فِرْدَوْس (firdaws), ultimately from Proto-Iranian *paridayjah.

ety: rel. n. fr. firdaus
def: s.m. Name of a famous Persian poet, the author of the great epic poem Shāh-nāma (he lived about A.D. 1000).
more: n. prop. Name of a celebrated poet.

ety: rt. of firebīdan, or fireftan = fariftan = fardaftan; rt. Zend dab with prep. fra = S. प्र + दभ् (दम्भ्, दभनोति)
def: part. adj. Deceiving, cheating; alluring, seducing, captivating, winning (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mardum-fareb, 'deceiving or betraying men'; — dil-fireb, 'heart-alluring'); — s.m. Deception, deceit, fraud, trick, duplicity, treachery, imposture, delusion, fallacy; allurement, beguilement, &c.
more: n. m. 1. ( In comp .) Alluring; winning, as dil-fareb . 2. Fraud ( dhokā-dihī ). fareb denā, yā k. , v. a. To cheat ( dagā denā ). fareb se, fareban , adv. Fraudulently; dishonestly; malâ fide ; unfairly. fareb khānā, fareb meṅ ānā, yā phaṅsnā . See dhokā khānā .

(prop. fireb, from فريفتن or فريبيدن) 1. s. m. Deceit, trick, deception, fraud, allurement. 2. part. act. (in compos.) Cheating, deceiving; as, مردم فريب mardum-fareb, Betraying men. فريب آميز fareb-āmez, adj. Deceitful. فريب خورده fareb-ḵẖụrda, adj. Deceived. فريب دينا fareb denā, To deceive, delude, allure, cheat, defraud, cozen, beguile. فريب کار fareb-kār, adj. Deceitful. فريب کهـانا fareb-khānā, To be deceived or deluded.
subs: adj. & s.m. Deceitful, cheating; alluring; — a cheat, &c.
part. adj. Deceived, cheated, duped, gulled
s.f. Deceiving, deceit, imposing upon, cheating, swindling
(-ko), To deceive, impose upon, cheat, delude, defraud, dupe, gull; to allure, beguile
adj. & s.m. Practising deceit or treachery, deceitful, fraudulent, treacherous; — an impostor, a cheat
s.f. The practice of deceit or treachery, deceitfulness, deceit, imposture, &c.
(-se, or -par), To be deceived or deluded (by), be taken in (by), be duped, be gulled, be cheated (by), be misled (by)
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فریب (fareb, firēb). First attested in c. 1692 as Middle Hindi فریب (fryb).

ety: imperf. part. of fireftan; see fireb
def: part. adj. & s.m. Deceiving; — deceived; — a deceiver.

ety: perf. part. of fireftan; see fireb
def: part. Deceived, deluded; seduced; infatuated; fascinated, charmed, enamoured; — s.m. & f. Deluded person; doter, &c.
more: adj. فريفتن to be enamoured. Enamoured; charmed; fascinated. farefta k. , v. a. To charm; fascinate. farefta h. , v. n. To be charmed, fascinated.
part. pass. (of فريفتن) 1. Deceived. 2. Enamoured, charmed, infatuated; doter.
subs: v.t. To delude; to seduce; to fascinate, charm
To be deceived or deluded (by); to be fascinated (by), to be enamoured (of, -kā), be madly in love (with).

ety: fir = far = Zend fra ( = S. प्र)+ ishta = Zend aśta, aś = S. अश् (अश्नोति)
def: s.m. An angel; a messenger; a missionary; an apostle; a prophet
more: n. m. فرستن to send. 1. An angel. 1. Tumhāre farishtoṅ ko bhī to khabar nahīṅ hai . Prov. Even your angels have no knowledge of this. 2. Wahāṅ farishtoṅ ke bhī par jalte haiṅ! Prov. Even the angels cannot get access there. 3. Pakṛe jāte haiṅ farishtoṅ ke likhe par nāhaq, Admī koī hamarā dam-i-tahrīr bhī thā? Gālib. Unjustly stigmatized upon recording angel's writ, What witness on my side appeared when he indited it? 2. ( maut kā farishtah ) The angel of death ( jam ). 1. Mere jān ko farishta lag gayā hai . Wom. 2. Marne kā to dar nahīṅ! kahīṅ farishta ghar na dekh le! Prov. You have not even the fear of death! mind, the destroying angel does not find you out! farishte khāṅ . A strong or powerful man. Woh to farishte-khāṅ kī bhī nahīṅ suntā. farishta-khū, yā sifat , adj. 1. Angelic; heavenly. 2. Godly; good. farishte dikhāī denā , v. n. To be on the point of death; to be dying. badī kā fa…
(orig. فرسته from فرستن To send) s. m. An angel, an apostle, prophet, missionary, messenger.
subs: or firishta-ṣifat, adj. Angel-tempered; angelic, heavenly; god-like, godly
ī denā (-ko), Angels to appear (to); to be on the point of death.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِرِشْتَه (firišta). First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi فرشتہ (frśth /⁠firiśta⁠/).

def: s.m. pl. (of firishta), Angels, &c.

ety: perf. part. of firistādan; and = istāda, q.v.+pref. fir = far = Zend fra = S. प्र
def: part. Sent; — s.m. One who is sent, a messenger; an ambassador, envoy, a legate.

ety: for A. فرقة firqat, v.n. fr. فرق; see farq
def: s.m. A distinct body or class (of men), a party, body, troop, company, society, class, sect, tribe, kind.
more: n. m. A tribe; sect; class (جماعت 1).
(from فرق) s. m. A sect, tribe, class, kind, troop, company, society.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِرْقَه (firqa), from Arabic فِرْقَة (firqa).

def: s.f. A dish resembling hasty pudding, made of ground rice, milk, and sugar (cf. A. furnī, 'thick bread,' &c.; rel. n. fr. furn, 'an oven').
more: n. f. A mess of milk and ground rice.
s. f. A dish resembling hasty pudding, ground rice and milk.

def: (Pers. فرني firnī, q. v.) s. (dakh.) A dish or sweetmeat made of sūjī, sugar, ghī, &c.

ety: v.n. fr. firoḵẖtaṅ; Zend rt. vać with prep. fra = S. प्र+वच्
def: s.f. Selling; sale
more: n. f. Sale; disposal. rokht k. , v. a. To sell ( bechnā ).
(from فروختن) s. f. Sale, selling. فروخت کرنا faroḵẖt karnā, To sell.

def: part. (of firoḵẖtan; see firoḵẖt), Sold.

ety: rt. of firoḵẖtan; see firoḵẖt
def: part. adj. & s.m. Selling; — seller (used as last member of compounds, e.g. mewa-farosh, 'fruit-seller'); — s.f. Selling, sale ( = faroḵẖt)
more: to sell. ( In comp .) Selling; seller; as Mevā-farosh . A fruit-seller. faroshin' dah , n. m. A vendor. faroshindah-i-majāzī , n. m. A licensed vendor.
part. act. (of فروختن or فروشيدن) in compos. Selling, seller; as, حلوافروش ḥalwā-farosh, A seller of sweetmeats, &c. ميفروش mai-farosh, A seller of wine.

ety: firosh, q.v.+anda = Zend aff. anṭ = S. अन्त्
def: imperf. part. & n. of agency (of firoḵẖtan), Selling; — one who sells, seller, vendor
more: part. act. (of فروختن or فروشيدن) Selling, one who sells, seller.

ety: firosh, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Selling (used in comp., e.g. mewa-faroshī, 'fruit-selling').

ety: for pīroz, peroz; Pehl. peroj; Zend pairi+raoćaṅh, rt. ruć; S. परि+रोचः, rt. रुच्
def: adj. Victorious, triumphant; successful, prosperous, fortunate, happy
more: (or پيروز) adj. Victorious, happy, fortunate. قيروزاثر fīroz-as̤ar, Victorious, conquering. فيروزبخت fīroz-baḵẖt, Victorious. فيروزمند fīroz-mand, Victorious. فيروز مندي fīroz-mand-ī, s. f. Victory, prosperity.
subs: or fīroz-baḵẖt, or fīroz-mand, adj. Victorious, conquering

ety: for pīroza, peroza, rel. n. fr. fīroz, q.v.; — the S. पेरोज is prob. formed fr. the Pers.
def: s.m. A turquoise (com. regarded as a stone that brings good luck).
more: n. m. S. पेरोज A turquois.
(Pers. پيروزه Sans. पेरोज ) s. m. A turkois.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فیروزه (fērōza).

ety: fīroz, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Victory, triumph; success, prosperity, good fortune; — (rel. n. fr. fīroza), adj. Of the colour of the turquoise, azure, blue.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فیروزی (fērōzī, “turquoise-coloured”), from Arabic فَيْرُوز (fayrūz), from earlier Persian پیروزه (pērōza, “turquoise”).

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.f. Fees.
'Puss! puss!'

ety: fr. fishāndan = afshāndan = shawāndan, caus. of shudan; rt. Zend shu with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+च्यु (च्यवते)
def: part. adj. & s.m. Scattering, strewing; shedding; spreading, expanding, diffusing; strewed, — scatterer, strewer, &c. (used as last member of compounds, e.g. ātash-fishān, 'scattering fire').
more: part. (of فشاندن) in compos. Strewing, scattering, shedding, spreading, expanding, strewed, scattered.

ety: perf. part. of fishāndan; see fishān
def: part. Scattered, strewed; shed, spilt; spread, diffused.
more: part. pass. (of فشاندن) Scattered, strewed, spread.

ety: fishān, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Scattering, strewing, &c. (used, like fishān, in comp., e.g. jān-fishānī, q.v.).
more: in compos. s. f. Strewing, shedding, scattering, spreading.

ety: inf. n. of فسق 'to quit the right way,' &c
def: s.m. Vicious or immoral conduct; sin, iniquity; impiety; falsehood; immorality; adultery; obscenity; impudence
more: n. m. فسق transgressing. Sin; adultery.
(Heb. To divaricate) s. m. Adultery, obscenity, impudence, iniquity, sin, impiety, falsehood.
subs: s.m. Sin and iniquity; vice and immorality; adultery, &c.

ety: fi = af = Zend aiwi = S. abhi+sos = soz? q.v.
def: s.m. &c. = afsos, q.v. s.m. Sorrow, grief, concern; regret; vexation; — intj. Ah! alas! alack! ah me! what a pity!
subs: afsos karnā (-kā, or par), To feel regret, sorrow, &c., to regret; to grieve, sorrow, or sigh (for, or because of), to lament, bemoan; to take to heart; to express sorrow or regret (for); to feel or to express pity (for).

def: s. m. Ribbon, tape. (Portug. fita).

ety: fr. Port. fita
def: s.m. Ribbon; tape.
part. = uftāda, q.v. part. adj. Fallen, lying flat or horizontally; lying waste or untilled (land); poor, wretched, helpless
more: part. pass. (contract. from افتادن) Fallen. See افتاده uftāda.

def: s.f. = uftādagī, q.v.

ety: for فتنة fitnat, quasi-inf. n. of فتن 'to burn'; to try by fire,' &c.
def: s.m. Trial, affliction, calamity, mischief, evil, torment, plague, pest (applied to persons as well as things, e.g. yěh laṛkā bhī baṛā fitna haī); — temptation, seduction; — discord, conflict, cabal, faction, civil war, sedition, revolt, mutiny; perfidy; sin, crime
more: n. m. فتن burned. See balvā . 1. Sedition; faction. 2. Revolt. 3. Evil; pest. fitnah uṭhānā , v. a. To raise a disturbance, sedition, etc. fitnah-angez, fitna-pardāz , adj. See daṅgaī , 2, 3. fitnah-aṅgez, fitnah-pardāz , n. m. See āg-lagāū , 2.
Hin. Wom. adj. Naughty; wicked. Yeh laṛkā bhī baṛā fitnā hai . Wom. gul-i-fitna , n. m. A species of mimosa or acacia bearing strong scented flowers.

(from فتن To try to tempt) s. m. 1. Calamity, mischief. 2. Sedition, mutiny. 3. Perfidy. 4. Sin, evil, pest. 5. Temptation, seduction. فتنه انگيز fitna-angez, A fomenter of disturbances, an incendiary, a mischiefmaker, seditious, turbulent, insurgent, &c. فتنه انگيزي fitna-angez-ī, s. f. Causing disturbances, seditiousness, mischievousness, &c. فتنه جو fitna-jo, Quarrelsome, seditious. فتنه ساز fitna-sāz, Mischiefmaking, mischiefmaker. فتنه سازي fitna-sāz-ī, s. f. The occasioning of disturbance or trouble. فتنه گر fitna-gar, A seditious person, a mischiefmaker.
subs: v.n. To raise a revolt or sedition, &c.
or fitna-pardāz, adj. & s.m. Mischievous, seditious, turbulent, insurgent, &c.; — a mischief-maker, a fomenter of disturbances, a turbulent fellow, an incendiary, &c.
adj. & s.m. Seeking disturbance, seditious; quarrelsome, turbulent; — a quarrelsome or turbulent fellow, &c.
adj. & s.m. Mischief-making, sedition-causing; — mischief-maker, a seditious person, &c.
s.m. A species of mimosa or acacia bearing flowers and having a powerful scent.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فِتْنَه (fitna), from Arabic فِتْنَة (fitna).

ety: for A. فطنة, inf. n. of فطن
def: s.f. = fat̤ānat, q.v.
s. (dakh.) Rice-milk of a thicker consistency than khīr (Qanoon-e-islam).

ety: v.n. fr. افطر aft̤ara, 'to break a fast,' iv of فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.m. The breaking of a fast; — the festival held on the breaking of the fast of Ramazān ( = īduʼl-fit̤r, q.v.s.v. ʻīd).
s.m. = fit̤rat, q.v.
s.m. Saddle-straps; cords fixed to a saddle for hanging game to.
more: s. m. Cords fastened to a saddle behind, meant for hanging game to; saddle-straps.

ety: for A. فطرة, v.n. fr. فطر; see fat̤r
def: s.f. Creation; production; — the natural constitution, or nature, or form (with which one is created); — nature, constitution; natural disposition or temper; a natural or innate quality or property; idiosyncrasy; — wisdom, sagacity, shrewdness, skill, ingenuity; astuteness, cunning; deceit, trick, artifice, device, plan, scheme, plot, intrigue, machination, stratagem; — (fr. fit̤r, q.v.) the alms of the breaking of the fast of Ramazān
more: n. f. فطر made. 1. Nature; creation. 2. Wisdom ( dānāī ). 3. Artifice; plot. 4. Intrigue; machination. 5. See upāë , 1, 4, 5. fitrat k. , v. a. To scheme; plot, etc. fitrat laṛānā , v. a. To plot and counterplot; to practise tricks or stratagems.

s. f. 1. Wisdom, sagacity, cunning. 2. Nature, creation, form. 3. Deceit, trick. 4. Alms given on the عيد الفطر īﻌdu-l-fit̤r. فطرت لڙانا fit̤rat laṛānā, v. a. (vulg.) To practise tricks or stratagems, to cheat, to shuffle.
subs: v.n. To exercise (one's) skill or ingenuity; to practise artifices or stratagems; to plot and counterplot; to cheat, to shuffle.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian فطرت (fitrat), from Arabic فِطْرَة (fiṭra, “human nature”).

def: adj. Sagacious, cunning.

ety: rel. n. fr. fit̤rat
def: adj. & s.m. Natural, constitutional; — sagacious, shrewd; cunning, artful, designing, crafty; — a cunning or artful person, &c.
more: adj. 1. Natural. 2. ( fitrat-bāz ) Designing; crafty ( chālāk , 3).

ety: for afzā, rt. & imperat. of afzūdan; Pārsī awazā; rt. Zend śā with prep. aiwi = S. अभि+शा (शो, श्यति)
def: part. adj. Increasing, augmenting, enhancing (used as last member of compounds, e.g. jān-fazā, 'life-increasing').
more: part. in compos. (from فزودن) Increasing. See افزا afzā.

ety: fizā, q.v.+ī = Zend aff. i = S. इ
def: s.f. Increasing, &c. (used, like fazā, in comp.).
s. (dakh.) Hammer.

ety: inf. n. of فحش 'to be gross, or lewd, or indecent,' &c.
def: s.m. Gross, foul, immodest, lewd, or obscene, speech or language; grossness, obscenity (in word or deed), ribaldry, bawdry; — adj. Gross, indecent, obscene, &c. (e.g. fǒḥsh bāteṅ, 'foul or indecent language;' fǒḥsh kitāb, 'an obscene book').
more: adj. Obscene; bawdy; indecent. fohsh , n. m. Obscenity; bawdry. fohsh bāteṅ, yā zabān . Obscene language. fohsh bāteṅ baknā, fohsh baknā , v. n. To talk smut or bawdy. fohsh sher . Obscene ballad or verse. fohsh kitāb . An obscene book. fohsh kitāboṅ kā bechnā . Sale of obscene books. fohsh gāliyāṅ , n. f. Indecent abuse. fohsh gīt . A bawdy song.
s. m. Obscenity (in word or deed), bawdry, grossness.
wety: From Arabic فحش (fuḥš).

def: s.m. = فوطه fot̤a, q.v.

ety: fr. fota = pota = S. पुट
def: s.m. A bag; purse; the scrotum, the testicles; — tax, revenue, collections
more: n. m. 1. The scrotum; testicles. 2. Taxes; revenue; collections. fota bharnā ; Rus. potā bharnā , v. a. To pay tax or revenue. fote chhiṭaknā , v. n. To be ruptured (the testicles). fote-khāna , n. m. A treasury ( khazāna , 1). fote-dār , n. m. Cashier; treasurer. fote-dārī The office of collecting tenant dues.

(Pers. پوته) s. m. 1. A bag. 2. The scrotum, the testicles. 3. Tax, revenue, a purse. فوطه دار fot̤a-dār (vulg. پو تدار) A banker, cash-keeper, cashier, treasurer. فوطه داري fot̤a-dār-ī, s. f. Bankership.
subs: (-meṅ), To pay a tax or revenue
vulg. fot̤e-dār, or fot̤-dār, s.m. Treasurer, banker, cashier, cash-keeper; a collector of tenant-dues
vulg. fot̤e-dārī, s.f. Bankership, treasurership; — the office of a collector of tenant-dues.

def: s.m. Valerian.

ety: rt. فأد
def: s.m. The heart.
